faqs.question: Can I get direct admission at GNDU?
faqs.answer: GNDU offers admission as per notification issued by Punjab Government. The University offers admission to its UG and PG courses on the basis of both merit and entrance-based. Admission to most of the courses is based on candidates performance in the Hence, the university does not offer direct admission. Aspirants can further reach out to the university admission office to get more details related to direct admission.

faqs.question: Can I get admission in GNDU without entrance exam?
faqs.answer:
Yes, for some course, GNDU takes admission without entrance exam. The admission to those courses is allotted based on the merit secured in the qualifying exam.  
Admission to following GNDU courses is based on merit in the qualifying exam:
UG Courses PG courses
BTech (Urban &Regional Planning) MEd
Bachelor of Tourism & Travel Management . PG Diploma in Mental Health Counseling
BA PG Diploma in Translation
BSc MCom, MSc Mathematics 
BPT MBA, MCA, MSc (IT), MSc (Maths), MA (Eco), MA Punjabi, MCom

faqs.question: Is BCA available in GNDU?
faqs.answer: Yes, the university offers a BCA course of three-year in a full-time mode. BCA and BCA (Hons) are offered by the university under the five-year integrated course in Computer Science. The UG course is also offered as an online course. Admission to these programmes is based on the entrance test conducted by the university. Many of the GNDU-affiliated colleges also offer this UG programme. Candidates who meet the eligibility requirements for this course can take the entrance exam.

college_info.intro:
The flagship programmes offered at GNDU include BSc (Hons), MSc, BTech & LLM, among others. After doing BSc, students can have a career in IT, Software Development etc. GNDU also has a Directorate of Open & Distance Learning (ODL) located at its main campus for distance & open programmes. By doing various courses from Guru Nanak Dev University, students can get job offers from top recruiters like Amazon, Accenture, Axis Bank etc.
In addition to this, Guru Nanak Dev University offers UG, PG, diploma, short-term, & certificate programmes across various fields eg Engineering, Science, Law, Arts & Humanities, Commerce & Accounting, Computers & IT, Management, Journalism, Architecture & Pharmacy, among others. Candidates can check the Guru Nanak Dev University course admissions details along with the eligibility criteria & the total course fee in the following table:

placements.overview: Guru Nanak Dev University gives placement offers to the students. As of 2026, 4 students of MBA Batch were placed in Crescendo Global. They received a package of INR 8.20 LPA. 23 BTech students were also placed in KPMG which is a company from the Big Fours. The key highlights of Guru Nanak Dev University placements 2025 are tabulated below:

fees_and_eligibility.overview: Guru Nanak Dev University courses are offered at the UG, PG, and doctoral levels. These courses are offered in streams such as Engineering, Medicine & Health Sciences, and Business and Management Studies, among others. GNDU admissions are based on the merit obtained in the qualifying exam as well as the course-specific entrance exam. Further, the Guru Nanak Dev University courses are offered to the students in full-time mode.

faqs.answer: A:    Guru Nanak Dev University BCom fee the biggest part is the tuition fees that costs between INR 26,000 and INR 3.2 lakh. Tuition fee is paid every semester. Other kind of fees includes admission fee, examination fee etc. For more details, also visit the GNDU website.
faqs.question: What is the fee for B.Com course at GNDU?

faqs.answer: A:    Yes, Guru Nanak Dev University's BCom admissions are entrance-based. Admission to all the courses offered at the university is based on a departmental-level entrance exam conducted by  the Head of the (respective) Department offering the programme. GNDU. To appear for this exam, candidates must fill out an application form on the official website of the university. Based on the performance in this exam, a merit list is prepared. The applicants who manage to get shortlisted are allotted seats, provided they present the require documents supporting their eligibillity.

reviews_list.batch_info: B.Sc. in Medical Science - Batch of 2024
reviews_list.review_title: No placement, affordable.

review_content.Placements: No placement is provided by the college. 30% of students get jobs by themselves. There are no big companies. No internship is provided by the college. This college is not for placement. It is only for getting a degree. In Science, we only get knowledge of Science theoretically.
review_content.Infrastructure: It was average. There was Wi-Fi at the start only. Laboratories were good. Classrooms were good. The library was good. The hostel was good. The canteen was not hygienic at all. Medical facilities were there. Sports and games were available in college. NCC was present. Overall, it was good.

reviews_list.batch_info: Bachelor of Computer Applications (BCA) - Batch of 2028
reviews_list.review_title: Good learning experience at GNDU with a supportive faculty.

review_content.Placements: Placements for the BCA course are okay and mostly depend on how much effort a student puts in. Around 40-50% of students get placed, and many find jobs on their own. The average salary is around 2-3 LPA. Internships are also available in small IT companies, and common roles are IT support, junior developer, and customer support.
review_content.Infrastructure: The college infrastructure is good and well-maintained. Classrooms and labs are properly set up and comfortable for regular use. Wi-Fi and library facilities are available for students. Hostel rooms are clean and manageable, and the food in the mess is decent. Sports, medical, and other basic facilities are also available, making campus life smooth and comfortable.

reviews_list.batch_info: B.A - Batch of 2026
reviews_list.review_title: I think this college can be improved with some changes.

review_content.Placements: I guess about 50% of students get placements around 1 LPA. I don’t know much about the exact figures. Around 60% of students get internships.
review_content.Infrastructure: Our college has Wi-Fi, labs, classrooms, and a library. The college does not provide hostels. Food quality is not up to the mark and can be improved. Medical facilities are good. For sports and games, the college is not as strong as some other colleges. There is room for improvement in the overall infrastructure.
review_content.Faculty: Teachers are qualified and very good. Their teaching is also good. Exams are kind of easy, and the pass percentage is around 55%. The college cannot fully make students industry-ready. Students have to gain practical experience themselves. Faculty can do better in this area.

reviews_list.batch_info: Bachelor of Business Administration (BBA) - Batch of 2025
reviews_list.review_title: Excellent college campus.

review_content.Placements: 50 percent of students are placed in our course. The highest package offered in our college is Lacs for the entire course and the lowest package offered is 1.2 LPA for the entire course. After completing, we can work in banks and various private sectors. 60 percent of students got internship from our course and are in Ram Financial Limited.
review_content.Infrastructure: The facilities and infrastructure of our college are good. There are Wi-Fi, smart boards, a library, and all other facilities available. The quality of the facilities in our hostel is good, with quality food and medical facilities available. We play indoor as well as outdoor games like cricket, volleyball, badminton, tennis, and carrom etc.

reviews_list.batch_info: Master of Computer Applications (MCA) - Batch of 2019
reviews_list.review_title: Review of Guru Nanak Dev University College, Jalandhar, GNDU,

review_content.Placements: The placement cell was a huge add-on to my career path. They instantly initiated the process of the placement and related stuff when I entered my 2nd last semester of the curriculum. They provided us companies for internships and full-time placements and introduced us to companies that are dream companies for many. Many companies come on campus, including local companies of the state, Byjus, Wipro, TCS, Amazon, Datalog, etc. The company with the highest package offers 14 Lacs. If you sit in placement, a minimum of 7.5 CGPA is required, and there were different criteria for placement.

reviews_list.batch_info: M.Com - Batch of 2023
reviews_list.review_title: They conduct less interactive classes, which are so boring.

review_content.Placements: RSM India, BDO India, S. R. Batlibboi College, and Grant Thornton Bharat LLP are a few of the auditing companies that have shown their interest in hiring a large number of candidates from our department. We only depend on the placement cell for internship offers in the nearby companies. Professional and skilled courses are also arranged for us to train us with proper skills.
review_content.Infrastructure: Only the exact number of students is admitted to all the courses to lessen the crowd and to improve the performance. Sports and other physical activities are scheduled only once a week. There are certain liberties in hostel premises and other things. We visit the library only during free periods and break hours.

Each aspirant must meet the course-specific eligibility requirements to be eligible for the course admission at the university. Candidates can pursue a wide range of programmes across multiple specialisations in a variety of fields at the university. The university accepts various national-level entrance tests. Furthermore, it also conducts its own entrance test for admission to some of the courses. Guru Nanak Dev University Amritsar seatintakevaries for each course. Students are offered seats based on their performance in the selection rounds. Candidates must pay the admission fee to secure their seat at the university. Students can look at the table below to know course-wiseGNDU seat intakefor some of its popular programmes.
Note:The information mentioned above is taken from official sources. Hence, may vary.

As per the information available on the official website of the University,GNDU admissionto various courses is either based on merit or an entrance exam, depending on the course selected. Foradmission to the BTechcourse, aspirants have to appear forJEE Main entranceand admission to the MBA course candidates are required to appear for theGNDU Management Entrance Test (GMET). For all courses, admission is done based on the merit secured by the candidates in the entrance exam conducted by the Head of the Department. Candidates seeking admission to GNDU courses must begin their admission process by filling out the application form before the deadline. The step-wiseGNDU admission processis given in the table below:
Step 1: GNDU application process
Candidates can apply online for the preferred course. Candidates have to fill out the application form and pay the application fees before the deadline.
Step 2: GNDU Selection Process

Admission to most of theGNDU courses is entrance-based. Only for diploma-level courses, the admission criteria is merit-based. Applicants applying to BTech and MBA courses are required to appear forJEE Main and GMET,respectively, to get shortlisted for admission. Apart from BTech and MBA, the University shortlists candidates based on theentrance exam conducted by the Head of the Department.
Step 3: GNDU Merit list and Admission confirmation

After completing the selection process,GNDU releasesa course-wise merit list on its official website. All the candidates, whose name is on the merit list, are required to appear for the counselling/ interview process for seat allocation and document verification process.

courses_data.description: Guru Nanak Dev University (GNDU) MBAis offered with multiple specialisation options such asFinance,HRM,Marketing, etc. The University Business School and the University School of Financial Studies offer the MBA course.GNDUMBA admission is based on merit in theGuru Nanak Dev University Management Entrance Test (GMET). Selected candidates are allotted seats through counselling. Candidates who have completed their graduation in any stream from a recognised University are eligible to apply for admission atGNDU MBA. More highlights related toGNDU MBAare mentioned below:

faqs.question: How can I take admission in GNDU to pursue MBA?
faqs.answer: Candidates seeking admission to GNDU MBA programmes need to take the Guru Nanak Dev University Management Entrance Test (GMET). The university shortlist candidates based on exam score. The selected candidates have to participate in the counselling during which the final seat allotment is carried out. Moreover, to apply for the entrance exam, candidates can visit the official website of Guru Nanak Dev University.

faqs.question: How will I know if I have been shortlisted for GNDU GMET counselling?
faqs.answer: The University will release a merit list of candidates selected for the counselling round on its official website soon after the declaration of GNDU GMET result. The shortlisted candidates will have to appear at the university main campus for interview rounds along with the required documents. Candidates must report at the alloted date and time, failing which candidate's request for change in date and time will not be entertained.

faqs.question: When will GNDU GMET registrations commence?
faqs.answer: Guru Nanak Dev University conducts GMET for admission to Management courses offered at its Main and regional campuses. The university usually opens registration for GMET in May and closes by second week of July. The exam is usually conducted in July every year in online mode for 150 minutes.

placement_data.title: Guru Nanak Dev University Placement Overview 2025
paragraphs: Guru Nanak Dev University has released the salary details for the batch passed out in 2025. The highest package secured by batch 2025 was INR 44 LPA , while the average package offered during GNDU placements 2025 was INR 6.37 LPA . The University has placed 84.89% students in reputed companies. The top recruiters of Guru Nanak Dev University in 2025 include Amazon, Accenture, Coca Cola, Capgemini, Dabur, IBM, etc.

Earlier, 652 students were placed during the 2024 placement session. The highest and average packages stood at INR 18.43 LPA and INR 6.40 LPA , respectively. KPMG, Accenture, Nucleus Software were among the few top recruiters of GNDU.

highlights.description: GNDU is one of India’s top institutes. It is ranked #12 by the India Today 2025 under the University (Government) category. It ranked #77 in the QS World Rankings 2026, internationally. institute is well-known for its well-structured academic curriculum, excellent faculty, and well-laid infrastructure. The GNDU rankings have been explained in the article below:

nirf.description:
GNDU is consistently recognized as one of the top institutes across all major categories by NIRF 2023. It has been noticed that in the 'Overall' category the institute's ranks have shown a fluctuating trend when evaluated against the ranking of the prior year. In 2023, GNDU fell 7 ranks to #87 rank in the 'Overall' category by the NIRF ranking.
It has been seen that in the 'University' category, the institute's ranking has fluctuation when compared with the previous year's ranking. In 2023, GNDU fell 4 ranks to #48 rank in the 'University' category by the NIRF ranking. In the 'University' category, GNDU ranked in the top 50 in the country for the second consecutive year in 2023. Students can refer to the table below for the GNDU NIRF rankings:
Read less

qs_world.description:
GNDU has received strong ranks by the QS World University Rankings under the 'Asia' category. Provided parameters including, Teaching, Graduation Outcome, Inclusivity and Research, the institute is ranked in a good position as compared to other institutes by the QS World University Rankings 2026. In the 'Asia' category, the institute's ranks have descended when compared with the previous year's ranking. In 2026, GNDU fell 43 ranks to #329 rank in the 'Asia' category by the QS World University Rankings. This is the second consecutive year that the institute has slipped in the rankings.
One can observe that within the category of 'Southern Asia', the institute's ranking has fallen when compared with the previous year's ranking. In 2026, GNDU fell 9 ranks to #77 rank in the 'Southern Asia' category by the QS World University Rankings. This is the second consecutive year that the institute has slipped in the rankings.

hostel_campus.college_name: Guru Nanak Dev University Amritsar Infrastructure Details & Reviews

hostel.description:
There are two messes and two canteens in the hostel and both are run on a contract basis. There are some indoor games facilities like table tennis, carom board, volleyball and two badminton courts are also available within the premises of the hostel for the students. The students have access to the internet through Wi-Fi in all the rooms. Girls hostel is well-equipped with facilities like the internet with wi-fi, medical room, utility shop, photo state, PCO-cum-stationary store, general store, reading hall, mess, canteen, etc. 
Local students from within a radius of 40 kilometres (25 kilometres in case of girls), evening students, employees and part-time students are not eligible for admission to the hostel.
facilities: Holds approximately 543,357 documents, including books, bound journals, theses, reports and manuscripts.
Subscribes to about 134 Indian journals, 52 foreign journals, and 24 newspapers, and has acquired over 15,600 e‑books.
Rare and manuscript collections include around 5,174 rare books and 1,511 manuscripts.

library.description:
The library remains open on all the days of the year except on two National Holidays i.e. 26th January and 15th August. It opens from 9 a.m. to 9 p.m. on weekdays and 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. on weekends and other holidays. 
Bhai Gurdas Library is subscribing 87 Foreign and 146 Indian Journals. In addition to it, a library is also receiving about 150 journals as a gift. The library is a member of INFLIBNET which is providing access to 15,000+ e-journals and six databases. The library is also subscribing more than 200 journals through IEEE. It have 1,516 rare manuscripts relating to Punjab History, Culture, Sikh Religion and other faiths. There are 22 departmental libraries also for the convenience of the students and teachers.

reviews.batch: Batch of 2024Offered by Saroop Rani Government College For WomenReviewed on 20 Jan 2026)
reviews.review_heading: No placement, affordable.
reviews.review_text: It was average. There was Wi-Fi at the start only. Laboratories were good. Classrooms were good. The library was good. The hostel was good. The canteen was not hygienic at all. Medical facilities were there. Sports and games were available in college. NCC was present. Overall, it was good.

reviews.batch: Batch of 2028Offered by Shri Guru Teg Bahadur College, SathialaReviewed on 20 Jan 2026)
reviews.review_heading: Good learning experience at GNDU with a supportive faculty.
reviews.review_text: The college infrastructure is good and well-maintained. Classrooms and labs are properly set up and comfortable for regular use. Wi-Fi and library facilities are available for students. Hostel rooms are clean and manageable, and the food in the mess is decent. Sports, medical, and other basic facilities are also available, making campus life smooth and comfortable.

reviews.batch: Batch of 2026Offered by Apeejay College of Fine ArtsReviewed on 31 Dec 2025)
reviews.review_heading: I think this college can be improved with some changes.
reviews.review_text: Our college has Wi-Fi, labs, classrooms, and a library. The college does not provide hostels. Food quality is not up to the mark and can be improved. Medical facilities are good. For sports and games, the college is not as strong as some other colleges. There is room for improvement in the overall infrastructure.

reviews.batch: Batch of 2025Offered by Lyallpur Khalsa CollegeReviewed on 26 Dec 2025)
reviews.review_heading: Excellent college campus.
reviews.review_text: The facilities and infrastructure of our college are good. There are Wi-Fi, smart boards, a library, and all other facilities available. The quality of the facilities in our hostel is good, with quality food and medical facilities available. We play indoor as well as outdoor games like cricket, volleyball, badminton, tennis, and carrom etc.

reviews.batch: Batch of 2019Offered by Guru Nanak Dev University College, Jalandhar, GNDUReviewed on 17 Dec 2025)
reviews.review_heading: Review of Guru Nanak Dev University College, Jalandhar, GNDU,
reviews.review_text: It was a very good course to learn about programming languages; they were very focused on practicals so we could learn effectively. Every month, they hold special sessions so students can share their innovative ideas, giving the basic and advanced knowledge of C, C++, Java, JavaScript, MySQL, etc. In the first two semesters, basic knowledge of computers is given along with the main course. Hostel facilities were available at reasonable charges, and the room sizes were good. The food was hygienic...

reviews.batch: Batch of 2023Offered by Apeejay College of Fine ArtsReviewed on 11 Dec 2025)
reviews.review_heading: They conduct less interactive classes, which are so boring.
reviews.review_text: Only the exact number of students is admitted to all the courses to lessen the crowd and to improve the performance. Sports and other physical activities are scheduled only once a week. There are certain liberties in hostel premises and other things. We visit the library only during free periods and break hours.

reviews.batch: Batch of 2025Offered by Baring Union Christian CollegeReviewed on 27 Nov 2025)
reviews.review_heading: Faculty members are well-qualified, but placement through this college seems next to impossible.
reviews.review_text: The college infrastructure is not bad. It is good enough to stay focused. Surroundings are also clean and tidy. There are no issues with the infrastructure because the classrooms are spacious. Moreover, the library also has a large volume of books in every niche.

reviews.review_heading: Review of Guru Nanak Dev University.
reviews.review_text: The facilities and infrastructure available for our course were good. Wi-Fi facility was available. The library was also present. Modern classroom and computer labs. Hostel quality was also good. All essential amenities were available in the college. Sports and games were encouraged by the University.

reviews.review_heading: MBA at GNDU: Fees, placement and faculty overview.
reviews.review_text: The infrastructure is decent for the course and hostel. Adequate lighting, ventilation and fans are available in classrooms and hostel rooms. The department also has Wi-Fi and projectors for students and teachers. There are ramps for wheelchairs as well.

reviews.batch: Batch of 2025Offered by Amritsar Group of CollegesReviewed on 25 Nov 2025)
reviews.review_heading: Campus life, hostel life was good, but some of the teachers were so rude.
reviews.review_text: Facilities and structures are normal, not that good and not too bad. It's like okay. Wi-Fi is available at every point in the college. Labs are there, but not for agriculture students because they don't have permission to pursue this course now.

reviews.review_heading: University for Punjabis only.
reviews.review_text: Superb infrastructure for sports. I am cutting a star because of the hostel. Hostels are not well-maintained, but otherwise, the grounds and libraries are top class. They provide various facilities to students, such as a gym, pool, etc. Many canteens are not so good, and the mess is very poor.

reviews.batch: Batch of 2024Offered by Golden Group of InstitutionsReviewed on 22 Nov 2025)
reviews.review_heading: A good campus life with decent facilities and helpful staff.
reviews.review_text: The infrastructure was average but good enough for daily classes. Labs had the basic equipment we needed, and Wi-Fi worked most of the time. Classrooms were clean and comfortable. The library was helpful, but it needed more updated books. Hostel rooms were okay, and the food was manageable. Sports and medical facilities were simple but useful when required.

reviews.review_heading: There are a lot of extra curricular activities like - shooting, swimming, etc.
reviews.review_text: Neat and clean hostel and good infrastructure. Hostel rooms are good and neat and clean. Washrooms are clean. There is also a lift in the hostel. Food in the mess is nice and can be eaten. There are many shops in the hostel where we get all our necessary things.

reviews.review_heading: I am absolutely satisfied with the faculty, placements, infrastructure and facilities.
reviews.review_text: Overall, the infrastructure of our department is good, and many more departments, such as UBS, Agriculture, and the UIT department, have better infrastructure. Moreover, we have a health centre on our campus which includes great and experienced doctors and helpful staff.

reviews.review_heading: Good Engineering College .
reviews.review_text: The facilities and infrastructure available for our course was extremely good. Wi-Fi was available in the college. Labs were well-equipped with instruments. Library was present in the campus. Hostel was also good it's has water cooler, decent washrooms.

reviews_list.review_title: The college is excellent. If you want to fulfill your dream, you can surely take admission here.

review_content.Placements: In my college, about 150 students were placed this year. The highest package was 75 LPA as a start, and the average package was around 45–50 LPA. Companies like Apollo Hospital, research centers, Medanta Hospital, etc. hire students. About 70–75% of students get internships, and the rest can do internships at the medical facility on campus itself. There are various roles available, such as doctors, nurses, etc.
review_content.Infrastructure: Almost all the necessary facilities are provided to students. We have laundry services and cleaning rooms in the hostel. The infrastructure is very good and well-maintained; it includes a library, an auditorium, and a games room. Everything is in good condition and has air conditioning. The food quality in the mess also offers variations.

reviews_list.batch_info: Bachelor of Medicine and Bachelor of Surgery (MBBS) - Batch of 2026
reviews_list.review_title: Review of United Institute of Medical Sciences.

review_content.Placements: It is a new college and there are only 3 MBBS batches. Placements are average. It is a very good college for students.
review_content.Infrastructure: The infrastructure is great. All facilities are good. Dissection hall is available. Research laboratories are very good. Hospital is also available. Hostel facilities are perfect. Mess is available with attached washrooms. Our campus is fully air-conditioned. Classrooms are well-furnished. Hostel rooms have beds with mattresses. Everything is good in this college.

reviews_list.batch_info: Bachelor of Medicine and Bachelor of Surgery (MBBS) - Batch of 2025
reviews_list.review_title: Our college has good infrastructure and experienced faculty.

review_content.Placements: Internships are easily available at our college, and all support, goals, and placement are available to students. There is a hospital for students to practise and work and learn skills in a friendly manner with very helpful doctors and medical support staff.
review_content.Infrastructure: Our college has free Wi-Fi. We have a mess, a gym, a study room, a common room, a playground, and a TV, and all the facilities are available at the hostel. The warden is there to guard us. There is a hot geyser available for water in our college. We have all the basic features, staff, and facilities all available for free.

individual_reviews.title: The worst college in Prayagraj.

content.Placements: They offer very low placements. The average package is between 3-6 LPA. It is a below-average college.
content.Infrastructure: They didn't provide any kind of Wi-Fi. During inspection everything was okay and after that, everything was worse. In summer AC never works. If they find that somewhere AC is working they cut the connection and most importantly during admissions, the exception area is chilled.
content.Faculty: They have 3rd-grade employees working for organizing especially the vice-chancellor. Initially, they treat students in a very genuine manner, but after one to two months, they treat them like students were their unpaid servants. They always blame students for minor things.

individual_reviews.course: B.Sc. in Nursing - Batch of 2025
individual_reviews.title: Infrastructure 10/10, Faculty 5/10, Placement 2/10.

content.Placements: Here no placement company comes to hire students, but students go to off-campus placements. Some students have offer letters to join the company, but they are not giving the NOC to go. No placement activity has been performed by them, and no fests, cultural, or sports events are here to have some fun.
content.Infrastructure: We have maintained and good infrastructure of the university and hostel as well, but the mess is so messy. United Institute of Medical Sciences registration process is online. Candidates must register for the UP NEET exam to take admission in the MBBS course. Application forms are available on the official website of UP NEET.
Candidates are selected for the MBBS course based on their scores in the UP NEET exam. They must appear for the exam followed by the counselling process for admission.

United Institute of Medical Sciences NEET UG cutoff 2024 has been announced for admission to the MBBS course. The institute offers admission to the Bachelor of Medicine and Bachelor of Surgery (MBBS) course based on candidates’ performance in the NEET UG exam. Eligible candidates are then called for the counselling rounds for final admission. During the United Institute of Medical Sciences 2024 cut off, the last round closing rank for MBBS admission was 74223. For more information, refer t

As per the United Institute of Medical Sciences MBBS cutoff 2024, the last round closing rank was 74223 for the General HS category students. The cutoff for the same course was 317867 in 2023 and 342946 in 2022 for admission. So, analysing the cutoff trends over three years, MBBS saw a drop in its last-round closing ranks for the students belonging to the General HS category.

United Institute of Medical Sciences accepts NEET UG scores for admission to the MBBS course. Candidates can refer to the table below to check out the important NEET UG dates for admission to United Institute of Medical Sciences.

The United Institute of Medical Sciences cutoff 2025 has been released for MBBS admissions across various categories belonging to the HS quota. Considering the UP NEET UG Round 4 cutoff 2025, admission for the General HS category for MBBS was closed at a rank of 76904.

reviews.review_heading: The worst college in Prayagraj.
reviews.review_text: They didn't provide any kind of Wi-Fi. During inspection everything was okay and after that, everything was worse.  In summer AC never works. If they find that somewhere AC is working they cut the connection and most importantly during admissions, the exception area is chilled.

reviews.review_heading: Infrastructure 10/10, Faculty 5/10, Placement 2/10.
reviews.review_text: We have maintained and good infrastructure of the university and hostel as well, but the mess is so messy. The choices for food and breakfast are good, but the taste is like "I forgot the real taste of that food." After many complaints, no changes have been made in the mess and chef.

reviews.review_heading: The college is excellent. If you want to fulfill your dream, you can surely take admission here.
reviews.review_text: Almost all the necessary facilities are provided to students. We have laundry services and cleaning rooms in the hostel. The infrastructure is very good and well-maintained; it includes a library, an auditorium, and a games room. Everything is in good condition and has air conditioning. The food quality in the mess also offers variations.

reviews.review_heading: Review of United Institute of Medical Sciences.
reviews.review_text: The infrastructure is great. All facilities are good. Dissection hall is available. Research laboratories are very good. Hospital is also available. Hostel facilities are perfect. Mess is available with attached washrooms. Our campus is fully air-conditioned. Classrooms are well-furnished. Hostel rooms have beds with mattresses. Everything is good in this college.

reviews.review_heading: Our college has good infrastructure and experienced faculty.
reviews.review_text: Our college has free Wi-Fi. We have a mess, a gym, a study room, a common room, a playground, and a TV, and all the facilities are available at the hostel. The warden is there to guard us. There is a hot geyser available for water in our college. We have all the basic features, staff, and facilities all available for free.

reviews.review_heading: Good faculty, placement offers, and good campus infrastructure.
reviews.review_text: The hostels are good, and all necessary facilities are available. The warden is sweet and friendly. Medical facilities are available. The mess provides food four times a day, morning, afternoon, evening, and night. My hostel also provides gym facilities and access to the sports complex.

reviews.review_heading: It is the best, affordable, and appropriate college.
reviews.review_text: Our campus is spread over 240 acres of land. We get every basic and advanced facility in our prestigious campus. It has centralized AC to a high-quality Wi-Fi network.

reviews.review_heading: I'm satisfied with the college, people, and facilities.
reviews.review_text: The infrastructure is structured and systematic for every course. Facilities are good, classrooms are clean. There are good air-conditioned rooms with projectors. Wi-Fi is not available, so students have to manage on their own. Labs are well-equipped, and the library has a good collection of books.

reviews.review_heading: My colleges infrastructure and facilities are world class and it has its own hospital .
reviews.review_text: The entire college campus is equipped with Wi-Fi and along with that there is a very clean facility, sports facility and a very big library where you can study comfortable. There are many mess and canteens inside the college where you can eat breakfast and food comfortable.

faqs.answer: A:    Students can enrol in remote learning programmes at University of Madras (Madras University) all year round, from the academic year (June to May) through the calender year (January to December). The Madras University Institute of Distance Education offers instruction in a variety of subjects, including science, the arts, business, management, and information technology (IT). The University of Madras founded Madras University Distance Education (UNOM IDE) in 1981. Admission to Madras University's distance learning programme is merit-based. The admission to the courses is done based on first-come-first-basis. Hence, admission to this college is not tough.

faqs.answer: A:    Yes, the Institute of Distance Education, the University of Madras refunds fees of addmission if necessary, but you have submitted an application with a valid reason and valid proof of addmission to get a refund. faqs.answer: The University of Madras IDE is well-known for offering distance education programs in fields such as:Business AdministrationComputer ApplicationsTourism and Travel ManagementPublic RelationsHuman Resource ManagementFinancial ManagementJournalism and Mass CommunicationExport and Import ManagementCyber SecurityDepending on the demand in the job market and the interests of the students, these programs' popularity can change. It is essential to visit the institute's official website and evaluate the course options, curriculum, and admission requirements for each programme to select which PG Diploma programme at IDE is the best fit for your career objectives and interests.
faqs.question: Q: Which PG Diploma course is Institute of Distance Education, University of Madras famous for?

faqs.answer: Students may apply for a distance learning course at Madras University both online and offline. Merit and entrance exams are used in the admissions process. While submitting an application to the University of Madras IDE, applicants must follow a few processes. The following is a list of the stores for the two procedures:For the online application: Applicants are required to visit the official website of the university.Register themselves through the admission portal. Select the desired course and fill the application form with the adequate details.Submit the application form with requisite documents and pay the application fees through online Net Banking or via Debit/Credit Card. For the offline application: Students can obtain an application form by visiting the college campus and have to pay INR 200 for the application form. Fill the application form with all the required academic & personal details and choose the desired course. Attach the requisite documents and submit the application form.Documents RequiredThe following Certificates (Original) should be enclosed while not fail :10th Mark List.Intermediate Mark List.PG Degree first Year Statement of Marks.UG Degree Certificate with Statement of Marks
faqs.question: Q: How can I take admission in Institute of Distance Education, University of Madras?

faqs.answer: More than 50 PG Diploma courses are available at Madras University, both on-campus and through affiliated colleges. There are three delivery options for the course: full-time, part-time, and online. Madras University offers PG Diploma programs with a one-year study length. This course's admission is merit-based. A candidate must hold a UG Diploma/Bachelors or equivalent degree from this University or from any other university recognised as equivalent thereto in order to be admitted to a PG Diploma programme at the University of Madras.Popular courses offered are:PG Diploma in Labour LawPG Diploma in Banking and FinancePG Diploma in Hospital ManagementPG Diploma in Technical WritingPG Diploma in Accounting and Finance

admission_overview.title: Madras University Distance Education (IDE UNOM) Admission Overview 2026
admission_overview.description: To apply to Madras University (IDE UNOM) Distance Education then you have to access the official IDE UNOM site at the web address of http://www.ideunom.ac.in at which the online application form will be available to apply to different undergraduate, postgraduate, and diploma programs. Once the online form has been completed and submitted, experts of the university on program will contact you to guide you in rest of admission process. Go to the Institute of Distance Education (IDE) Web Site of Madras University: Visit the Official IDE UNOM Web Site.. Locate the Online Application Portal: You have to find the admissions or application section and you will see the online application forms of the different courses.

admission_process.title: Madras University Distance Education Admission Process 2025

paragraphs: Madras University Distance EducationAdmission 2025offers a range ofUG and PG coursesin the streams of Arts and Humanities, Commerce, Science, IT, Management, and Performing Arts, such as BA, BCom, BSc, BCA, BBA, and Bachelor of Literature in Tamil (BLit in Tamil), all with a duration of three years. Additionally, Madras University Distance Education Admission 2025 ensures students benefit from a dedicated placement cell providing training, placements, and internship opportunities. UG admission is merit-based and is done on first-cum-first-serve basis.

For Madras University Distance Education admissions to variousUG & PG courses,candidates have to fulfil the minimum eligibility criteria, followed by the other selection rounds. Find below the course-wise eligibility and selection criteria related to Madras University Distance Education course admissions 2025:

Madras University Distance Education fees may include various components such as Tuition Fees, Admission Fees, etc. Candidates have to pay a certain amount of fees at the time of admission. The below table mentions the Madras University Distance Education fees for various UG & PG courses:
Madras University Distance Education admissions 2025 are merit-based. Candidates have to qualify based on the last qualifying exam. Find below the detailed process for MUD admissions 2025:
Step 1: Madras University Distance Education Application Process 2025

Madras University Distance Education admission process 2025 starts with the application process. Candidates can download the application process from the university's official website. The duly filled application form has to be sent either through post or submitted in person at the concerned department at MUDE. However, the application and entrance test fee will be paid online and candidates have to attach the pay slip along with the two copies of the online bank challan with the application form.
Step 2: Merit List Release

After the completion of all the selection rounds, based on merit. The university will release the final list of selected candidates. Students who find their names on the merit list need to pay a certain amount of fee to secure their seat.

reviews.review_text: The college hostel is conveniently located within the campus and boasts excellent infrastructure. It offers 50 spacious rooms, each capable of accommodating three students comfortably. The rooms are well-maintained and equipped, ensuring a comfortable living environment. The rooms are meticulously maintained, and hygiene standards are upheld. Each floor boasts 20 bathrooms and restrooms for residents' convenience. Priority for hostel location is given to applicants residing farther from Chennai due to limited seating availability. The annual rental fee is set at an average of 10,000 rupees, while the monthly mess bill is payable separately.

review_content.Placements: 1. All 250 seats were filled in my batch. 2. Since it is a medical college, there is no concept of placements. 3. No highest, lowest, or average package offered in our course. 4. However, during the internship, the stipend increases 2000 every year. For 2028 batch, it would be 26-28000 .
review_content.Infrastructure: 1. Our college has been granted an NAAC 'A+' grade and has been conferred with ISO 9001: 2015 certification. 2. The infrastructure is top-notch, with not a speck of dirt lying anywhere. 3. The Wi-Fi is quite fast, with a few exceptional days. 4. The labs and classrooms are updated and the college has all state-of-the art equipments. 5. The library is quite possibly one of the best aspects of our university, considering of the advanced facilities. It has a card-wiping technology for issue of books and different floors for UG, PG, Residents, and Doctors. 6. The girls hostel are very requisite, with AC/non-AC rooms available, with a personal bathroom and small kitchen available. 7. The boys hostel is a bit far from the University but is as nostalgic and fun. 8. The mess and canteen food is nice and affordable. 9. Our hospital offers excellent medical facilities, being free for the students. 10. Our university has 2 gyms, a basketball court, a tennis court, a volleyball and handball court, and a football/cricket arena .

faqs.answer: The undergraduate MBBS programme at Kempegowda Institute of Medical Sciences (KIMS) admits 150 students annually. It is not unlawful to enter KIMS directly for MBBS. Students who want to pursue medical education but have poor NEET scores can apply for management quota seats.Yes, you can join (KIMS) directly through the NEET exam. Admission to the undergraduate MBBS program at KIMS is based on NEET scores, and there are no restrictions on joining directly if you meet the eligibility criteria. review_summary.college_name: KIMS Karad Reviews on Placements, Faculty & Facilities

individual_reviews.course: MBBS - Bachelor of Medicine and Bachelor of Surgery - Batch of 2026
individual_reviews.title: KVV is good place for students to study.

content.Placements: After completing MBBS, you will surely get a job as a tutor for Rs 60,000 per month and as a medical practitioner for 1 lakh per month. KVV pass out students have secured good jobs in a hospital. You will get a good job if you just work on your skills. As a doctor, your skills should be good.
content.Infrastructure: Hostels are good. There are 2 types of hostels, like AC hostels for Rs 2,00,000 and non-AC hostels for Rs 70,000. Non-AC hostels have common washrooms, whereas AC hostels have attached washrooms. Hostel food is good. Both non-veg and veg are served. Breakfast, lunch, and dinner are provided. Environment is good for students to study. Library is provided and the environment is great for students.

individual_reviews.course: BDS - Bachelor of Dental Surgery - Batch of 2025
individual_reviews.title: Great campus life awaits.

content.Placements: Excellent dental college for undergraduation. College itself provides internship and externship programmes for students. A total of 96 students were present in my course when I joined. College provides postgraduate facilities in all clinical subjects for students.
content.Infrastructure: Classrooms are AC and well-equipped with all audio-visual aids required for teaching. Infrastructure is great with all amenities on campus itself. The mess serves delicious food and maintains all hygiene. The campus has a swimming pool, gym, badminton court, as well as a basketball court. On-campus, there is also an amphitheatre, salon, and bazaar.

individual_reviews.course: MBBS - Bachelor of Medicine and Bachelor of Surgery - Batch of 2028
individual_reviews.title: It is a very good college in terms of medical college.

content.Placements: 1. All 250 seats were filled in my batch. 2. Since it is a medical college, there is no concept of placements. 3. No highest, lowest, or average package offered in our course. 4. However, during the internship, the stipend increases 2000 every year. For 2028 batch, it would be 26-28000 .
content.Infrastructure: 1. Our college has been granted an NAAC 'A+' grade and has been conferred with ISO 9001: 2015 certification. 2. The infrastructure is top-notch, with not a speck of dirt lying anywhere. 3. The Wi-Fi is quite fast, with a few exceptional days. 4. The labs and classrooms are updated and the college has all state-of-the art equipments. 5. The library is quite possibly one of the best aspects of our university, considering of the advanced facilities. It has a card-wiping technology for issue of books and different floors for UG, PG, Residents, and Doctors. 6. The girls hostel are very requisite, with AC/non-AC rooms available, with a personal bathroom and small kitchen available. 7. The boys hostel is a bit far from the University but is as nostalgic and fun. 8. The mess and canteen food is nice and affordable. 9. Our hospital offers excellent medical facilities, being free for the students. 10. Our university has 2 gyms, a basketball court, a tennis court, a volleyball and handball court, and a football/cricket arena .

individual_reviews.course: MBBS - Bachelor of Medicine and Bachelor of Surgery - Batch of 2025
individual_reviews.title: An amazing college with great staff members, infrastructure, and facilities.

content.Placements: Strength of batch is 250 students. The college does not provide placements. The college provides internships at the hospital of the university, Krishna Hospital, and the stipend for the interns is Rs 18000 per month. Hostel and mess fees are excluded.
content.Infrastructure: The campus is located in the heart of karad city at very good location with nice atmosphere all around the area of 60 acres more or less. The college has modern-era infrastructure with separate buildings for various departments of dental, nursing, MBBS, physiotherapy, etc.

individual_reviews.course: MBBS - Bachelor of Medicine and Bachelor of Surgery - Batch of 2024
individual_reviews.title: Satisfactorial experience uptill. Could work on the management and time tables.

content.Placements: The internships which are supposed after the fourth year are PAID unlike many other deemed institutions. Ratio of cracking competitive entrance exams after the courses are pretty good. There are multiple courses available for future education. NAAC-A+ grade.
content.Infrastructure: Newly developed high-tech library with more than over 5000 books excluding medical magazines, research papers etc. Campus Wi-Fi is supported with high-speed internet that works smoothly on laptops etc. Greenery of the campus is one of the calming zone in chaotic schedule.

admission_process.title: KIMS Karad Admission Process 2025

paragraphs: KIMS Karad Karad admission 2025 is open for all UG, PG, doctoral and diploma courses. Admission to the university is both entrance and merit-based. InKrishna Vishwa Vidyapeeth, the application mode is online. The university is also known asKrishna Vishwa Vidyapeethand offers education in the field of medicine and healthcare. The university offers courses under various faculties including Medical, Dental, Nursing, Physiotherapy, Pharmacy and Allied Sciences.Krishna Vishwa Vidyapeethprovides several programmes includingMBBS, BSc, BPharma, MDS, and MS and others. Admission toMBBSis granted based on the score obtained in theNEETentrance exam. students can apply for MBBS admission to the university by applying on the official website of NEET. All the information related toKIMS Karadadmission along with courses is available in the below section:

faqs.question: What is the seat intake for B.Sc at KIMS?
faqs.answer: Medical Science undergraduate, graduate, and doctorate programs are available at KIMS. It is a reputable university for medical science. It provides 100 seats for B.Sc. Different courses have various payment schedules. The first year of B.Sc costs INR 92000/-, while the second year costs INR 82000/-. Thus, qualified applicants ought to enroll at this university.

faqs.question: Does KIMS Karad offer BSc?
faqs.answer: Yes, KIMS Karadoffers a four-year, full-time BSc  programme at the UG level. Candidates pass Class 10 and Class 12 or equivalent with Physics, Chemistry, English and Biology with 50% aggregate taken together in Physics, Chemistry and Biology from a recognised board are eligible to apply for admission in the BSc programme. The university offers various specilisation in BSc and offers merit and entrance based admisson.

The candidates will be shortlisted based on their past academic records and scores obtained in the NEET UG.

faqs.question: Can I join KIMS Karad without entrance?
faqs.answer: No, you can not join KIMS Karad without entrance exam. For admission toMBBS, BDScourses, a candidates must have passed Class 12 with Science subjects from any recognised boards, and secured a valid scoreNEET. ForBSc and MSc (Nursing)admisison, candidates requires to appear for different exam (state level or college-level).

faqs.question: Can I join KIMS directly?
faqs.answer: The undergraduate MBBS programme at Kempegowda Institute of Medical Sciences (KIMS) admits 150 students annually. It is not unlawful to enter KIMS directly for MBBS. Students who want to pursue medical education but have poor NEET scores can apply for management quota seats.Yes, you can join (KIMS) directly through the NEET exam. Admission to the undergraduate MBBS program at KIMS is based on NEET scores, and there are no restrictions on joining directly if you meet the eligibility criteria. Additionally, there are management quota seats available for students with low NEET scores who wish to pursue medicine at KIMS

placement_report.college_name: KIMS Karad Placement & Salary Insights

placement_data.title: KIMS Karad Placement Overview 2024
paragraphs: As per Krishna Vishwa Vidyapeeth NIRF report 2024, the median package offered during UG 4-year and UG 5-year placements 2023 stood at INR 6.82 LPA and INR 9.72 LPA , respectively. Further, the median package offered during PG 2-year and PG 3-year placements 2023 stood at INR 5.63 LPA and I NR 14.1 LPA , respectively.

Earlier, according to the NIRF report 2023, 88 BDS, 17 MDS, 78 MBBS and 74 MD/ MS students were placed during 2022 placements. The median package offered to BDS and MDS students was INR 5 LPA and INR 13 LPA , respectively. For MBBS and MD/ MS , the median package offered during KIMS Karad placements 2022 was INR 9.25 LPA and INR 13.10 LPA .
KIMS Karad Placements 2022:

data: KIMS Karad Placements 2022: A drop of nearly 29% was recorded in the number of BDS students placed in 2022 when compared with 2021 The median package offered to MDS students in 2022 increased by over 8% when compared with 2021

tables.context: What was the highest package offered at KIMS?

faqs.question: What was the highest package offered at KIMS?
faqs.answer: The KIM Placements 2023 are now complete. While the average KIM package climbed by 6% to INR 8.50 LPA, the top KIM package jumped by 24% to INR 18 LPA. Participating in the placement drive were recruiters from Kirolskar Group, PwC, Deloitte, HUL, Marico, Reliance Retail, and other companies. the highest package INR 18 LPA

faqs.question: Who are the top recruiters of KIMS?
faqs.answer: The KIM Placements 2023 are now complete. While the average KIM package climbed by 6% to INR 8.50 LPA, the top KIM package jumped by 24% to INR 18 LPA. Participating in the placement drive were recruiters from Kirolskar Group, PwC, Deloitte, HUL, Marico, Reliance Retail, and other companies.KIMS is famous for MBBS and provides almost 100% placements for MBBS degree. KIMS Karimnagar has an active placement cell that ensures placement for deserving students through campus interviews facilitated by leading national and international companies

faqs.question: How are MBBS placements at KIMS?
faqs.answer: Hello Aspirants, If you are a KIMS student at the time of recruitment then you can expect a good job because MBBS students here get some good placements. Last year, around 80 medical students got a job with a package of approximately INR 10 LPA. The highest monthly salary received by an individual student was INR 80,000 per annum. KIMS has some good networks that benefits their students. KIMS students after completing their education goes on to work for reputed hospitals and healthcare institutions including government and private establishments, as well as NGOs. The institution's dedicated placement cell further aids students in their job search, offering support such as career counselling, resume writing assistance, and interview preparation. Hope this information helps you and if you have any further questions please come back.

faqs.question: Does KIMS offer better placements than College of Nursing, Sangli, Bharati Vidyapeeth?
faqs.answer: Each university has its own advantages. KIMS is a highly esteemed university. However, Bharti Vidyapeeth is equally well-known. Bhartiya Vidyapeeth institution, which has two campuses, is a deemed institution in comparison. While KIMS University only provides 100 seats, it offers 156. Bharti Vidyapeeth is one that I will recommend if you choose to choose one. In general, each university is superior at its particular level. Placement is better at Bharti Vidyapeeth.

description: KIMS Karad cutoff 2025 is available on this page for various accepting exams. Candidates can refer to the exam-wise sections below to view the cutoff details across different rounds and categories. Other important updates are as follows:

KIMS Karad NEET UG cutoff 2025 was concluded with the release of the final seat allotment result for admission to the MBBS course. The cutoff was released for several categories belonging to the AI quota. For the General AI quota, the NEET UG last round closing rank was 300127 for MBBS admission. Candidates can refer to the table below to view the first and last round cutoffs 2025 for the General AI category students.
KIMS Karad MBBS cutoff 2025 was 300127 for the students belonging to the General AI quota. For other categories and rounds, use the provided filters.

KIMS Karad accepts the NEET UG entrance exam for selecting students to the MBBS course. Candidates with a valid score are then eligible for the NEET UG counselling. For 2026 admission, aspirants must refer to the table below to view some of the important NEET UG 2026 dates to be eligible for admission.

Candidates preparing for admission can refer to the table below to view or download the NEET UG round and year-wise consolidated cutoff list. This will help students to understand the past three years' cutoff trends.

KIMS Karad NEET PG cutoff 2025 has been released for admission to various PG-level courses, like MS. The cutoff was released in the form of opening and closing ranks for multiple categories under the All India quota. For round 4, the closing cutoff for the General AI category was 123404. According to this range, the most competitive course is MS. For more details, see the table below.
According to the KIMS Karad MS cutoff 2025, the most sought-after specialisation for the General AI category was MS in Ophthalmology. The round 4 closing rank for this specialisation was 123404.
Students can refer to the table below to know NEET PG cutoffs across different categories, courses, and years.
Students who appeared for the NEET PG exam 2025 can refer to the table below to view the NEET PG Round 1 cutoff list. They can also download the results for later use.

KIMS Karad NEET SS Cutoff 2024 was released for the concluding round in the form of ranks for admission to DM, M.Ch. courses across multiple specialisations. The cutoff was released for various categories belonging to the AI quota.
In the first round, KIMS Karad DM cutoff 2024 ranged from 393 to 2581 for students in the General AI category. was the toughest to get into. turned out to be the most easiest for admission.
In this section, candidates can find out the category-wise NEET SS cutoff 2024 and 2023. In 2024, for the General AI quota, the NEET SS cutoff was closed at 402 for DM in Critical Care Medicine.

The institute has been ranked #151-200 by the NIRF 2025 under the category Overall, and #102-125 under Pharmacy. The institute has been ranked well by eminent ranking bodies because of its curriculum, infrastructure and well-qualified faculty. Over the years, KIMS Karad has earned commendable rankings across different domains in the NIRF 2025. This signifies the institution's esteemed reputation and proficiency in various aspects, assigning it an overall good score. KIMS Karad's rankings in 'Overall' category have exhibited stability in trend as per the rankings of the previous years. In 2025, KIMS Karad's rank is #151, the same as last year's rank in the 'Overall' category by the NIRF ranking.
The rankings of the institute in the 'Pharmacy' category have displayed a decreasing trend when evaluated against previous year's rankings. In 2025, KIMS Karad fell 35 ranks to #102 rank in the 'Pharmacy' category by the NIRF ranking.

reviews.review_heading: This is a great college life one can have.
reviews.review_text: It provides Wi-Fi all over the campus, fully Wi-Fi zoned areas, high-tech laboratories, food quality was great. Students get breakfast, lunch, and dinner of good quality. People won't get bored of meals as they used to get a variety of meals. The mess will never disappoint students.

reviews.review_heading: Review of Krishna Institute of Medical Sciences.
reviews.review_text: KIMS has both air-conditioned and non-air-conditioned hostels. Recently a new air-conditioned hostel has been opened which is amazing and very hygienic. The warden is quite strict, and the security is also good. Recently the campus has been renovated. The library is the best part about the campus, which I don't think any other campus can provide that.

reviews.review_heading: Our college provides good infrastructure and facilities.
reviews.review_text: Our college provides facilities like an air-conditioned hostel, gym, swimming pool, cafeteria, air-conditioned library, and good hospital. Everything is available here. Hostels are good. Everything is nearby. The food served in the hostel is good. Classrooms, library, and everything are good and newly renovated.

reviews.review_heading: It provides excellent infrastructure and various facilities for students.
reviews.review_text: Wi-Fi services are very good. Library provides all types of books related to the course and reference magazines. The seating arrangement in the library is good and comfortable. Sports, gymnasium, and various outdoor games are available on the campus.

reviews.review_heading: Review of Krishna Institute of Medical Sciences.
reviews.review_text: There is free Wi-Fi on the whole campus. The library has unlimited Wi-Fi, air-conditioner, computer zone on each floor, separate floors for UG and PG students along with 10000 books and journals, and they provide online resources also. Hostel facilities are good, and both air-conditioner and non air conditioner rooms are available.

reviews.review_heading: There is good infrastructure and teaching faculty members. There is a scope for improvement.
reviews.review_text: Wi-Fi, labs, classrooms, library and all are good here. High facilities are provided. The library is sprawled over 3 floors and has air-conditioning and drinking water. Free Wi-Fi is available to all students. The food served in the mess is just amazing. It's clean and healthy as well as tasty. The price of the food in the mess is also affordable.

reviews.review_heading: Our campus is nice, but teachers don't teach much. The library is new.
reviews.review_text: The infrastructure is not much great. The campus is great. The library is fine, and the food court is nice. A sports week is organised, and it is average. The campus doesn't organise a lot of curricular activities, etc. It doesn't organise fests and gatherings after Covid-19 also. So, overall, our college doesn't provide good exposure. Still, the hospital OPD works well. The previous cafeteria was better. The food court is newly built.

reviews.review_heading: The college has really good infrastructure and provides fairly good education.
reviews.review_text: The infrastructure is amazing. It has got NABH+ accreditation. You can find the pictures on the website. It has a library with centralized AC and Wi-Fi. The hostel and mess facilities are available. The girls' hostel is on the campus itself, and but the boys' hostel is a little far from the college.

reviews.review_heading: It has the best infrastructure, campus, and a good crowd.
reviews.review_text: It has the best infrastructure. It is one of the cleanest campuses in India. The library is amazing. Our college also provides a gym, pool, basketball courts, cafeteria, well-equipped labs, museums, etc. The campus is Wi-Fi enabled. Hostels are very good. Both air-conditioned and non-air-conditioned rooms are available in the hostel.

reviews.review_heading: Posh high quality education for MBBS, BDS, nursing, physiotherapy, and academics are main power.
reviews.review_text: Classrooms are very good. Campus area is literally the best. But for only boys hostel of UG's its not that good. Light goes anytime, not that frequently. Central Wi-Fi is present. Library is top class. Food is also decent. For MBBS students, medical facilities are kind of free. Games and sports are not that much focused.

The NEET UG 2026 registration window is now closed for admission to the MBBS, BDS, and other courses. The exam is scheduled for May 3, 2026.

A few more updates are given below:

college_info.intro:
JNMC offers courses at the UG and PG levels while being a primary player in the field fo Medical and Research. The college offers these courses for 2 to 5.5 years on the basis of valid entrance exam scores. Candidates must secure a minimum aggregate and meet the eligibility criteria to secure admission at JNMC.
Furthermore, the college offers placement and internship opportunities through its hospital and placement training through various institutional collaborations. Furthermore, the course curriculum is designed by industry experts who are now active faculty members of the college. For more details on JNMC popular programmes, check out the table below:

fees_and_eligibility.overview: Jawaharlal Nehru Medical College AMU offers a full-time MBBS programme at the undergraduate level. At the PG level, the College offers in and an in specialisations. Additionally, the college offers a PG Diploma in one specialisation, namely Ophthalmology. At the super-speciality level, JNMC AMU also offers MCh in Plastic and Reconstructive Surgery. The seat intake for is , whereas only are offered under the . is entrance-based. Admission to all the programmes offered by JNMC is conducted in online mode only. For admission to the MBBS programme, a candidate should have completed of age as on of the year of admission.

faqs.answer: A:    Jawaharlal Nehru Medical College MBBS admission is entrance-based. The basic eligibility requires students to pass Class 12 or equivalent from a recognised board. Further, students need to appear and clear the NEET UG exam to qualify for the course.  Those who fulfil the above-mentioned eligibility may apply for the course by completing the admission process for the same.
faqs.question: Who is eligible for JNMC MBBS admission?

faqs.answer: A:     Jawaharlal Nehru Medical College accepts National Eligibility Entrance Test (NEET UG) scores for admission to its MBBS program. The minimum eligibility criterion for applying to the MBBS programme is to have 50% marks in 10+2 with Physics, Chemistry, Biology/Biotechnology, and English as the core subjects. The final selection for the MBBS course is based on NEET score and performance in the interview. For MD/MS/PG Diploma programs, admission is based on the qualifying marks obtained in NEET PG, and for MCh admission, it is based on NEET SS conducted by NBE.
faqs.question: What entrance exam scores does Jawaharlal Nehru Medical College accept for admission to MBBS?

faqs.answer: A:    Jawaharlal Nehru Medical College (JNMC) has different fees depending on the course and quota. For example, the MBBS course at JNMC Aligarh has a government fee of ?46,340 per year, while the MBBS course at JNMC Belgaum has a total tuition fee of ?82.3 lakhs. Tuition fees in government medical colleges for MBBS are generally lower compared to private colleges.
faqs.question: Is Jawaharlal Nehru Medical College more expensive than Medical College?

faqs.answer: A:    Yes, NEET PG scores are taken into account to get admission into Jawaharlal Nehru Medical College. NEET PG (National Eligibility cum Entrance Test for Post-Graduation), is conducted for providing admission to various MD (Doctor of Medicine)/ MS (Master of Surgery) and PG Diploma courses across the country.

reviews_list.review_title: Infrastructure of college is good.

review_content.Placements: There were 150 students until this year, but there is a proposal to increase the number of seats. The average salary package offered was about 12 LPA in hand after MBBS from this college as a junior resident. There are almost 100% of internships as it is compulsory in MBBS. Top role is offered after this course depending on PG branch.
review_content.Infrastructure: A Wi-Fi facility is available. Library is very good, which includes the issuing of medical books for a year. Classrooms are good and air-conditioned. Canteen is very bad and unhygienic; even the mess and food quality are not so good, especially for vegetarians. There is no computer lab.

reviews_list.batch_info: Bachelor of Medicine and Bachelor of Surgery (MBBS) - Batch of 2025
reviews_list.review_title: It has good clinical exposure. Our college and hostel life is better.

review_content.Placements: I have never seen any students getting a degree from here unemployed or having a less pay scale. Most students opt for post-graduation, and the majority of them get a seat in any specialization. Although very few candidates search for work in a private setup after MBBS and they also get a decent amount.
review_content.Infrastructure: The infrastructure is very good, and students can feel the beauty of nature. Classrooms are air-conditioned, practical laboratories are fine, and demonstration rooms are better because 20-30 students are taught there at a time. The hostel infrastructure is good with a big playground, average canteen, fine dining, and rooms are good.

overview_faqs.answer: Hostel Fee Structutre:For MBBS: INR 1000 (Per month including Mess charge.Rooms are neat, clean, and spacious, with a separate bed table and chair and a separate almirah. overview_faqs.question: What are people's opinions on Jawaharlal Nehru Medical College for MBBS?
overview_faqs.answer: Jawaharlal Nehru Medical College (JNMC) has received positive reviews for its academic quality, faculty, and facilities. JNMC Belgaum is considered a remarkable college for learning clinical skills, with a good reputation and excellent placements due to its association with a very good hospital. Similarly, JNMC Aligarh is regarded as a prestigious medical college, ranking in the top 10 medical colleges in India and being part of a famous university.

individual_reviews.course: Bachelor of Medicine and Bachelor of Surgery (MBBS) - Batch of 2027
individual_reviews.title: A college that moulds you into a hardworking and good clinician.

content.Placements: My college is a 1962-built institute, so being an old and central institute, it harbours a very large number of patients every day, and being in MBBS, the more patients we see, the more knowledge and experience we gain. If you are a good clinician, you can work anywhere you want.
content.Infrastructure: We have a lecture theatre for theory classes, labs for all the laboratory skills, and different teaching and seminar rooms for teachers in the hospital wards. The lecture theatre has various technological advancements. A Hostel facility is available, which is quite good. Food is average. Canteens are available.

individual_reviews.course: Bachelor of Medicine and Bachelor of Surgery (MBBS) - Batch of 2028
individual_reviews.title: JNMC: The college of my dreams .

content.Placements: In MBBS there isn't a concept of placement but yes, for PG almost all the students get a seat in JNMC or other colleges because of the 50% internal seat quota. Many students have cleared USMLE as well and are well settled. The passing rate for NEET PG and INI CET is quite high.
content.Infrastructure: The campus is small but beautiful. It is connected to JNMCH through a long corridors. The labs are well-equipped. The book bank is quite well-equipped with ample of books. The hostel is good with decent mess food. We have 2 canteens which also provide affordable and good food.

individual_reviews.course: Bachelor of Medicine and Bachelor of Surgery (MBBS) - Batch of 2024
individual_reviews.title: Infrastructure of college is good.

The average salary package offered was about 12 LPA in hand after MBBS from this college as a junior resident. There are almost 100% of internships as it is compulsory in MBBS. Top role is offered after this course depending on PG branch.
content.Infrastructure: A Wi-Fi facility is available. Library is very good, which includes the issuing of medical books for a year. Classrooms are good and air-conditioned. Canteen is very bad and unhygienic; even the mess and food quality are not so good, especially for vegetarians. There is no computer lab.

individual_reviews.course: Bachelor of Medicine and Bachelor of Surgery (MBBS) - Batch of 2025
individual_reviews.title: It has good clinical exposure. Our college and hostel life is better.

individual_reviews.course: Bachelor of Medicine and Bachelor of Surgery (MBBS) - Batch of 2026
individual_reviews.title: It has good infrastructure, well-qualified teachers, beautiful campus, and Wi-Fi connectivity.

content.Placements: A good number of MBBS pass-outs clear the very difficult PG entrance exam called NEET PG INI and CET for admission in post-graduate courses in prestigious colleges like AIIMS and other best colleges. All the students tend to do internships after their final year.
content.Infrastructure: Our campus has good and Wi-Fi enabled. The whole university has Wi-Fi. The labs in our college are fully-equipped with the instruments required, and all the lab assistants are eager to help. The lecture theaters are spacious enough with a seating capacity of around 200 people, well-furnished and well-conditioned with enough air conditioners. The library is very spacious where a maximum of 200 students can study at a time. It is a very well place with zero disturbance with air-conditioners. The hostel environment is very friendly. Seniors are very helpful. Food is like our home food. Sports are given special importance. All the competitions are regularly held.

admission_overview.title: JNMC Admission Overview 2024
admission_overview.description: JNMC admissions are primarily entrance-based. The college accepts NEET-UG, NEET-PG, NEET-SS for admission to specific courses. The college offers MBBS at UG, MD/MS/PG Diploma at PG, and MCh at Super Specialty (SS) level all under one roof. The institute also offers a Master of Public Health (MPH) as an additional academic program.

admission_process.title: JNMC Admission Process 2025

paragraphs: JNMC admissionsare primarilyentrance-based. Jawaharlal Nehru Medical College AMU (JNMC AMU) accepts NEET=UG, NEET-PG, NEET-SS, etc. for admission to specific courses. The college in affiliation withAligarh Muslim University (AMU)offers MBBS at UG, MD/MS/PG Diploma at PG, and MCh at Super Specialty (SS) level all under one roof. The institute also offers a Master of Public Health (MPH) as an additional academic program. Interested students can read the following article to know all about the courses, eligibility, and other information related toJawaharlal Nehru Medical College admission.

JNMC course admissionis entrance-based. The college offers undergraduate, postgraduate, super speciality and doctoral programmes in streams such as Medical, Nursing, Applied Science, and more. Students must meet the eligibility and selection criteria of the desired course prior to applying. Candidates can check belowJNMC coursesalong with their eligibility and selection criteria:

Jawaharlal Nehru Medical College feesinclude different components such as tuition fees, one-time payments, alumni registration fees etc. Selected candidates need to pay the admission confirmation fee to secure their seats at the institute. The fee varies as per the course and specialisation selected by the students.JNMC course feesrange betweenINR 97,300andINR 2.3 lakh. Refer to the following table to know about theJNMC feefor some of its courses:
Note: This information is taken from official sources and is subject to changes.

Jawaharlal Nehru Medical College seat intakevaries as per the programme chosen by the student. To secure a seat at the institute, candidates need to participate in the counselling process conducted by the institute. Find below the JNMC seat intake for popular courses:
Note: This information is taken from official sources and is subject to change.

Theadmission process for each JNMC coursediffers from another one. However, the steps remain common which are to register for the course-specific entrance, appear for the exam-specific counselling process and then final seat allotment for the shortlisted students. Find below the step-by-stepJawaharlal Nehru Medical College admission processexplained below:
Step 1: JNMC Application Process
JNMC application process differs with every course offered by the college.
Step 2: JNMC Selection Process
JNMC shortlists the students based on their performance in the entrance exams. Students need to register for theNEET counsellingfor seat allotment.
Step 3: JNMC Admission Confirmation
Post the final seat allotment, selected candidates who want admission can submit the fee and confirm their admission.
lists: MBBS:Students seeking admission to MBBS are required to apply via the NEET UG entrance exam.

faqs.question: Can I get into Jawaharlal Nehru Medical College with a NEET UG cutoff rank of 1000?
faqs.answer: Yes, if you belong to the General AI quota, you can get into Jawaharlal Nehru Medical College with a NEET cutoff rank of 1000. In 2025, the cutoff ranks ranged between3971and12762,so there are high chances that candidates will get a seat at Jawaharlal Nehru Medical College. However, it is not mandatory for a candidate to get a seat in the initial round, and therefore, they are advised to wait till the last round to confirm their chances of admission.

faqs.question: What is the cutoff for admission at Jawaharlal Nehru Medical College?
faqs.answer: Jawaharlal Nehru Medical Collegeaccepts NEET UG scores for the admission to the MBBS course for General All India quota. For the Round 3 seat allotment, theJNMC cutoff 2024for the MBBS course is3752for the General AI category candidates. While comparing the previous years' cutoffs, a decrease in the cutoff trend can be visible in the Round 3 seat allotment results for MBBS course. The total tuition fee for the course isINR 97,300, whereas the seat intake is 151. Some of the MD courses offered at the college include General Medicine,Pathology, and Microbiology, among others. faqs.question: How much rank should I score to get a seat at Jawaharlal Nehru Medical College?
faqs.answer: Jawaharlal Nehru Medical College NEET PG cutoff 2025was released for round 3 for various AI categories for admission to the MD and MS courses.For the General AI category, the MD cutoff ranged from339to102708, withMD in Radiodiagnosisbeing the most difficult to gain admission to.For the MS course specialisations, the round 3 cutoff ranged from2465to12283, whereinMS in Obstetrics and Gynaecologywas the most competitive for admission.

faqs.question: Is MBBS admission at Jawaharlal Nehru Medical College based on merit or entrance exam?
faqs.answer: MBBS admission at JNMC is strictly based on the NEET entrance exam, which is also merit-based. Students must not only qualify but also secure a competitive rank. Seat alotment is determined by NEET scores, counseling preferences, and available seats, making both entrance performance and merit essential.

faqs.question: Is NEET the only way to get an MBBS seat at JNMC?
faqs.answer: Yes, NEET-UG is mandatory for MBBS admission at JNMC. There are no direct or management quota entries. All candidates, including reserved category students, must qualify NEET and participate in the official counseling process.
