admission_process.title: Arundathi Institute of Medical Sciences and Hospital Admission Process 2025

paragraphs: Arundathi Institute of Medical Sciences and Hospital admission 2025is open for the MBBS program. The institute offers the MBBS course and conducts the application process through an online mode. Admission to the MBBS program is entrance-based.Arundathi Institute of Medical Sciences and Hospitalis affiliated with KNRUHS, Warangal, and followsKaloji Narayana Rao University of Health Sciencesadmission norms for its courses. All the information related to Arundathi Institute of Medical Sciences and Hospital admission, along with course eligibility and selection criteria, is available in the article below:
Arundathi Institute of Medical Sciences and Hospital Admission 2025: Eligibility and Selection Criteria

Admission to the MBBS program at Arundathi Institute of Medical Sciences and Hospital is entrance-based. Candidates must qualify for the NEET UG examination and participate in the Telangana NEET UG counselling conducted by KNRUHS. The table below lists theArundathi Institute of Medical Sciences and Hospital courseeligibility and selection criteria:
Eligibility
Selection Criteria
- Class 10 and Class 12 with Physics, Chemistry, Biology, and English with 50% aggregate for the general category (40% for reserved category)
- The candidate should have completed the age of 17 years as on or before 31st January of the year the candidate shall be appearing for the NEET-UG examination.
NEET UG+ NEET UG counselling, conducted by KNRUHS

The admission process for Arundathi Institute of Medical Sciences and Hospital in 2025 is conducted through the NEET UG examination and counselling.Candidates must complete the following steps for admission:
Step 1: NEET UG Application
Candidates seeking admission to the MBBS program must register and appear for the NEET UG 2025 examination conducted by theNational Testing Agency (NTA).
Step 2: NEET UG Counselling Registration
After qualifying for NEET UG, candidates must register for the Telangana NEET UG counselling conducted by KNRUHS.This involves submitting necessary documents and preferences for colleges.
Step 3: Seat Allotment and Admission

Based on NEET UG scores and counselling, candidates will be allotted seats.Upon receiving a seat allotment at Arundathi Institute of Medical Sciences and Hospital, candidates must pay theArundathi Institute of Medical Sciences and Hospital feeand complete the necessary formalities to confirm their admission.

headings.text: Steps for Arundathi Institute of Medical Sciences and Hospital Admission Process 2025

courses_data.name: Arundathi Institute of Medical Sciences and Hospital MBBS Admissions 2025
courses_data.description: Arundathi Institute of Medical Sciences and Hospital offersMBBScourses over 4 and a half years divided into four phases. A total of 150 seats are available for the course. Candidates can check the eligibility and selection criteria below:

faqs.question: Can I get admission to the MBBS course at Arundathi Institute of Medical Sciences and Hospital without NEET-UG?
faqs.answer: No, NEET-UG is a mandatory entrance exam for MBBS admission at Arundathi Institute of Medical Sciences and Hospital. Candidates must appear for NEET-UG, qualify the exam, and go through the Telangana NEET-UG counselling process conducted by KNRUHS for final seat allotment.

faqs.question: How are seats allotted for MBBS admission at Arundathi Institute of Medical Sciences and Hospital?
faqs.answer: Arundathi Institute of Medical Sciences and Hospital alloted seat for MBBS based on NEETIUG score. The allotment process is done through the Telangana NEET-UG counselling conducted by KNRUHS. Candidates are required to choose their preferred colleges during the counselling, and based on their NEET-UG rank and availability of seats, they will be allotted a seat.

faqs.question: How can I know if I am selected for MBBS admission at Arundathi Institute of Medical Sciences and Hospital?
faqs.answer: Candidates who clear the NEET-UG exam and participate in the Telangana NEET-UG counselling conducted by KNRUHS will be informed about their seat allotment through the counselling portal. Once the seat allotment list is released, candidates must check for their name and the allotted college. If candidates are shortlisted in the merit list, they will be required to report to the institute for document verification and fee payment to confirm their admission.

courses_data.name: B.Sc. Admissions 2026
eligibility: Accepting Exams:TS EAMCET, CBSE 12th, ISC, TSBIE

faqs.question: Does Arundathi Institute of Medical Sciences and Hospital accept Class 12 marks for admission?
faqs.answer: Arundathi Institute of Medical Sciences and Hospital accepts Class 12 with 50% aggregate for admission to MBBS courses. Further, to be eligible for admission candidates are required to apply and appear for the NEET-UG entrance exam, followed by counselling.

faqs.question: What is the eligibility criteria for admission at Arundathi Institute of Medical Sciences and Hospital?
faqs.answer: To be eligible for admission to MBBS courses offered at Arundathi Institute of Medical Sciences and Hospital, candidates must meet the following criteria:Pass Class 10 and Class with Physics, Chemistry, Biology, and English as core subjects.A minimum of 50% aggregate marks for General category candidates,  40% for SC/ST/OBC candidates, and 45% for PwD candidates.The candidate should have appeared for and qualified in the NEET-UG examination.The candidate must be at least 17 years old by December 31 of the year of admission.

faqs.question: How does Arundathi Institute of Medical Sciences and Hospital select students for admission?
faqs.answer: Arundathi Institute of Medical Sciences and Hospital admission to the MBBS course is entrance-based. Candidates must qualify the NEET-UG examination conducted by the National Testing Agency (NTA). Admission is offerd through the centralised counselling process conducted by Kaloji Narayana Rao University of Health Sciences (KNRUHS), Warangal. Candidates are required to register for counselling and select Arundathi Institute of Medical Sciences and Hospital as one of their preferences. Additionally, candidates must fulfill the basic eligibility criteria, such as pass Class 10 and Class 12 with 50% aggregate and score well in NEET-UG. If candidates meet all eligibility conditions and successfully complete the counselling process, they will be considered for admission.

faqs.question: Which entrance exams are accepted by Arundathi Institute of Medical Sciences and Hospital?
faqs.answer: Arundathi Institute of Medical Sciences and Hospital accepts NEET UG for admission to the MBBS programme. Candidates are required to achieve a high percentile in the NEET UG due to the high competition. Once the candidates are shortlisted, they must also appear for the counselling process conducted by theKaloji Narayana Rao University of Health Sciences. Interested candidates can visit the official website to know more about the courses, entrance exams and eligibility.
